info.title: Kirim.Email SMTP API Documentation
info.description: Kirim.Email SMTP Domain and credential management API Documentation

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of SMTP credentials for a domain

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain name that must be owned by the authenticated user. The domain must be verified and properly configured for email sending.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of credentials per page

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number

success.description: Indicates if the request was successful

data.description: Array of credential objects with pagination metadata

domain.description: Domain name for which credentials are listed

post.description: Create a new SMTP credential for a domain

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain name that must be owned by the authenticated user. The domain must be verified and properly configured for email sending.

success.description: Indicates if the request was successful

password.description: Generated password for the credential. Store this securely as it cannot be retrieved later

remote_synced.description: Whether the credential was successfully synchronized with the remote SMTP server

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain name that must be owned by the authenticated user. The domain must be verified and properly configured for email sending.

post.description: Comprehensive email validation including RFC compliance, DNS checks, and blocklist validation. Results are cached for performance.

post.summary: Validate a single email address with strict mode
post.description: Strict email validation including RFC compliance with no warnings, comprehensive DNS checks, and blocklist validation. Results are cached for performance.
Batch email validation with comprehensive checks. Optimized with caching - only uncached emails are processed. Maximum 100 emails per batch.

emails.description: Array of email addresses to validate (max 100)
Array of email addresses to validate (max 100)

post.summary: Validate multiple email addresses with strict mode
post.description: Batch strict email validation with comprehensive checks. Uses RFC validation with no warnings, comprehensive DNS checks, and blocklist validation. Optimized with caching - only uncached emails are processed. Maximum 100 emails per batch.

emails.description: Array of email addresses to validate (max 100)
Array of email addresses to validate (max 100)

200.description: Batch strict email validation completed successfully

parameters.name: end

parameters.name: sender

parameters.name: recipient

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of logs to stream

schema.description: SSE stream with log data batches and completion events

post.summary: Send transactional email for user domain with optional attachments
post.description: Send a transactional email for a specific domain owned by the authenticated user. This endpoint is designed for user-authenticated requests where the user owns and manages the domain. Supports comprehensive email customization, bulk sending, file attachments with advanced processing, and asynchronous job handling for optimal performance. Perfect for building custom email solutions and transactional workflows.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain name that must be owned by the authenticated user. This domain must be verified and configured with SMTP credentials.

requestBody.description: Complete email configuration including content, recipients, and optional attachments with processing options.

from.description: Sender email address. Must belong to the authenticated domain. Used for envelope sender and Return-Path header.

from_name.description: Optional sender display name. If not provided, will use the email address only.

to.description: Recipient email address(es). Single email string or array of emails for bulk sending. Maximum 1000 recipients per request.

subject.description: Email subject line. Supports Unicode characters, emojis, and variable content. Keep under 78 characters for optimal display across email clients.

text.description: Required plain text content for the email body. This will be used as fallback for email clients that don't support HTML. Line breaks will be preserved.
text.example:
Dear Valued Customer,

Thank you for your recent order #12345. Your items are being prepared for shipment.

Order Details:
- Product: Premium Widget
- Quantity: 2
- Total: $49.99

Tracking information will be sent once your order ships.

Best regards,
Customer Service Team

html.description: Optional HTML content for the email body. If provided, this will be used for HTML email clients. Should be a complete HTML document or HTML fragment.
html.example:
Order Confirmation - #12345

Dear Valued Customer,

Thank you for your recent order #12345. Your items are being prepared for shipment.

Order Details:

  • Product: Premium Widget
  • Quantity: 2
  • Total: $49.99

Tracking information will be sent once your order ships.

Best regards,
Customer Service Team

headers.description: Optional custom email headers. Can be provided as an object or JSON string. Common use cases include tracking headers, priority settings, and unsubscribe links.

reply_to.description: Reply-to email address. Must share the same top-level domain as the 'from' address for security compliance.

attachments.description: File attachment(s). Single file or array of files. Maximum 10 files per email, 20MB per file, 25MB total payload. Supports documents, images, and archives with optional processing.

attachment_options.description: Advanced attachment processing configuration as JSON string. Enables compression, password protection, and watermarking features.

202.description: Email send jobs successfully dispatched for asynchronous processing

success.description: Request processing success status

message.description: Detailed success message including recipient count, job batching information, and attachment count
message.example: Email send jobs dispatched for 5 recipients in 1 job(s) with 2 attachment(s)

post.summary: Send transactional email for user domain using a saved template
post.description: Send an email for a domain owned by the authenticated user using a stored template. Supports variable replacement using {{variable}} syntax in both subject and content. Template must belong to the authenticated domain. Supports attachments with the same processing options as non-template message endpoint.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain name that must be owned by the authenticated user

template_guid.description: UUID of the template to use

to.description: Recipient email address(es). Single email string or array of emails for bulk sending. Maximum 1000 recipients per request.

from.description: Sender email address. Must belong to the authenticated domain. Defaults to noreply@{domain} if not provided.

from_name.description: Optional sender display name. If not provided, will use the email address only.

variables.description: Variables for template replacement using {{variable}} syntax. Can be single object for all recipients or array matching recipient count

oneOf.description: Single set of variables for all recipients
Array of variable objects, one per recipient

headers.description: Optional custom email headers. Can be provided as an object or JSON string. Common use cases include tracking headers, priority settings, and unsubscribe links.

reply_to.description: Reply-to email address. Must share the same top-level domain as the 'from' address for security compliance.

attachments.description: File attachment(s). Single file or array of files. Maximum 10 files per email, 20MB per file, 25MB total payload. Supports documents, images, and archives with optional processing.

attachment_options.description: JSON string with attachment processing options. Watermark text is limited to 50 characters.

message.example: Template-based email jobs dispatched for 2 recipients in 1 job(s)
